guardrail_evaluations.explanation: This deal has no list price or proposed price — it is a rev-share partnership with $0 subscription fee, so the computed discount is 0%, well within the 25% PLG gate. However, the standard PLG discount guardrail is structurally inapplicable to a rev-share model; RevOps routing has already occurred per the AE's notes.

With no subscription price to discount, this rule does not fire on its face. The real economic exposure is the white-label access and feature pre-release — both of which carry opportunity cost not captured by a discount_pct metric.

Because the proposed price is $0 for Clay's platform access, there is no subscription revenue line from which to calculate a margin — the margin_pct on the base contract is effectively undefined (0%). The deal is economically a rev-share arrangement; until those rev-share payments materialize and exceed COGS attributable to white-label infrastructure, this structure cannot be certified as margin-positive against the 25% floor. This rule technically fires and requires CFO/exec resolution before close.

Same structural issue as the absolute floor — no fixed revenue means computed margin starts at 0% and grows only as rev-share is remitted. Yellow flag in force until a minimum guaranteed payment floor is contractually established.

alternative_structures.label: Minimum Guarantee + Rev-Share Blend
alternative_structures.description: Introduce a $120K/yr minimum annual platform fee (payable quarterly, non-refundable) that acts as a margin floor, offset against rev-share obligations above a $480K gross threshold. Reverberate still participates in full upside above the offset threshold; Clay locks in a known margin floor from day one.
alternative_structures.rationale: A $120K floor at assumed 40% list margin covers Clay's COGS carry for the white-label infrastructure. Reverberate benefits because the floor reduces their variable rev-share obligation once they hit scale — net cost to them at $800K gross is only $120K fixed + ~$80K variable vs. $200K all-variable. This clears the block_absolute margin guardrail with a verifiable revenue line.

alternative_structures.label: Tiered Rev-Share with Usage-Based Escalator
alternative_structures.description: Replace the flat 25% rev-share with a tiered structure: 20% on first $500K of Reverberate Engine ARR, 28% on $500K–$1M, 33% above $1M. No subscription fee. This rewards Clay more as Reverberate scales while giving Reverberate a lower cost of entry.
alternative_structures.rationale: At the $800K midpoint scenario, tiered rev-share yields ~$214K vs. $200K under flat 25% — a ~7% ACV uplift. More importantly, it aligns incentives: Reverberate's unit economics improve at lower tiers, making customer adoption easier, and Clay's take rate grows as the partnership proves out. Still carries margin uncertainty (no guaranteed floor), so this should be paired with a minimum guaranteed quarterly true-up payment.

alternative_structures.label: Platform Access Fee + Co-Marketing as Currency
alternative_structures.description: Charge a $75K/yr white-label platform access fee in exchange for reducing the rev-share rate to 18%. Bundle in a formal co-marketing commitment: 4 joint case studies/yr, logo placement, and a GTM-ops webinar series. Early access window shrinks from 30 to 14 days but is formalized in contract. Effectively monetizes brand and distribution value explicitly.
alternative_structures.rationale: The $75K platform fee is booked as subscription revenue at ~40% margin, immediately resolving the block_absolute guardrail. Reverberate's total cost of partnership at $800K gross rev-share scenario drops to $75K + $144K = $219K vs. $200K under the original proposal — a modest premium they'd accept in exchange for the formalized co-marketing rights and reduced variable rate. Clay gains guaranteed revenue, margin compliance, and 4 documented case studies with top-50 customers annually.

pricing.reasoning_summary: This deal has no list price, no proposed price, and no subscription fee — standard discount and margin guardrails cannot be evaluated against numeric thresholds in the conventional sense, producing a computed effective_discount_pct of 0% and a margin_pct_estimate of 0%. The gross margin absolute floor (block_absolute, ≥25%) technically fires because there is no verified subscription revenue line; this must be resolved at the CFO/exec level before close. All margin estimates in the alternative structures assume the standard working assumption that list price carries a 40% gross margin — the CFO should validate COGS attributable to white-label infrastructure before approving any structure. No precedent deals were directly applicable (highest similarity was 62%, all priced subscription deals), so alternative structures are constructed from first principles around the rev-share economics.

performance_obligations.name: White-label license to Clay tables and waterfalls (Reverberate Engine)
performance_obligations.description: Right for Reverberate to package and resell Clay's tables/waterfalls technology under their own 'Reverberate Engine' brand to end clients.
performance_obligations.rationale: The underlying functional IP is distinct and separately identifiable; because consideration is a sales-based royalty (25% rev share) on the licensee's subscription sales, ASC 606-10-55-65 sales/usage-based royalty exception applies — recognize as the underlying sales occur.

performance_obligations.name: Co-marketing rights and case studies with client attribution
performance_obligations.description: Right to jointly market and publish case studies referencing Reverberate's clients.
performance_obligations.rationale: Co-marketing is promotional and likely not a separately identifiable promise to the customer; if deemed distinct, it would be ratable over the 24-month term, but more likely accounted for as a marketing arrangement (ASC 606-10-25-19 not met for separate identifiability).

performance_obligations.name: Early access to new Clay features (30-day advance)
performance_obligations.description: Reverberate receives access to new features 30 days before public launch.
performance_obligations.rationale: Early access is an attribute of the underlying license rather than a separate promise; not separately identifiable from the white-label IP grant under ASC 606-10-25-21.

variable_consideration_flags.explanation: 100% of consideration is a 25% sales-based royalty on Reverberate Engine subscription revenue. Under ASC 606-10-55-65, royalties on licenses of IP are recognized when the subsequent sale occurs — no upfront estimation/constraint analysis is required, but period-by-period reporting from Reverberate is essential.

Joint marketing where Clay receives attribution to Reverberate's clients could be viewed as a marketing benefit; needs assessment of whether any cash/credits flow either direction and whether it is a distinct service per ASC 606-10-32-26.

Because end customers contract with Reverberate (not Clay) and Clay receives only a 25% share, Clay is likely the agent/licensor rather than principal for end-customer revenue. Only the 25% royalty is Clay's revenue (ASC 606-10-55-36 through 55-40).

contract_modification_risk.explanation: Early-access feature scope and the IP licensed under 'tables and waterfalls' will evolve over the 24-month term; material changes to the IP scope, rev-share %, or addition of new product lines would constitute a contract modification under ASC 606-10-25-10. Termination or renegotiation rights tied to the partnership's commercial performance also create modification risk.

recognized_revenue_schedule.notes: Midpoint estimate of $400K–$1.2M annual royalty range; recognized as Reverberate's end-client subscription sales occur (sales-based royalty exception). Actuals will vary.
Midpoint estimate; recognized as underlying sales occur. No upfront royalty estimation required under ASC 606-10-55-65.

red_flags.label: No baseline fee — 100% variable consideration
red_flags.explanation: TCV/ACV are $0 with all economics dependent on Reverberate's downstream sales. CFO and auditor should pre-clear revenue treatment, principal-vs-agent conclusion, and disclosure approach before signing.

red_flags.label: Principal vs. agent determination
red_flags.explanation: White-label structure where end customers contract with Reverberate suggests Clay is licensor/agent for gross subscription revenue; only the 25% rev share should hit Clay's top line. Needs formal memo.

red_flags.label: Royalty reporting and audit rights
red_flags.explanation: Quarterly net-45 remittance with no audit-rights clause noted creates risk of under-reported royalties and revenue cutoff issues at quarter-end.

red_flags.label: Channel conflict / cannibalization
red_flags.explanation: 8 of Clay's top-50 customers are Reverberate clients; white-label could cannibalize direct ARR, affecting standalone selling price analysis and any future bundled deals with the same end customers.

red_flags.label: Co-marketing and early-access promises
red_flags.explanation: Need to confirm these are marketing/relationship terms rather than distinct performance obligations to avoid inadvertent allocation of consideration.

asc606.reasoning_summary: This is a pure sales-based royalty white-label partnership with no fixed consideration, triggering the ASC 606-10-55-65 royalty exception and a non-trivial principal-vs-agent analysis. Multiple non-cash promises (co-marketing, early access) require distinctness assessment, and the 24-month term combined with evolving IP scope creates contract modification risk. This is an automated estimate; final determination requires CFO/auditor review.

flagged_clauses.customer_proposed_language: Reverberate would white-label Clay tables and waterfalls under their own branded product 'Reverberate Engine', presenting Clay's core infrastructure as their own product to end clients.
flagged_clauses.risk_explanation: An unrestricted white-label grant allows Reverberate to obscure Clay's brand entirely, undermining Clay's direct sales motion with 8 top-50 customers who are Reverberate clients and may never know they're on Clay. This also sets a precedent for agency resellers to commoditize Clay's product surface under third-party brands, eroding pricing power and brand equity. If Reverberate is ever acquired or pivots, the white-label license travels with them absent explicit restrictions.
flagged_clauses.suggested_counter: White-label rights shall be limited to: (a) the specific Clay features enumerated in Exhibit A (Tables and Waterfalls only, no API or data layer access), (b) use solely within active client engagements managed by Reverberate Growth during the Term, (c) subject to Clay's then-current brand-attribution guidelines requiring 'Powered by Clay' attribution in-product at no smaller than 10pt font in the product UI, and (d) non-transferable and non-sublicensable. Reverberate may not represent to any end client that the underlying technology is proprietary to Reverberate. Clay reserves the right to audit compliance with attribution requirements upon 10 business days' written notice.
flagged_clauses.fallback_position: Accept removal of mandatory 'Powered by Clay' in-product attribution in exchange for (i) Clay logo placement in Reverberate Engine marketing materials and sales collateral, and (ii) explicit contractual prohibition on Reverberate representing the technology as self-built or proprietary.
flagged_clauses.precedent_notes: Clay has not previously executed a white-label partnership agreement; this is a novel deal structure requiring legal and executive sign-off.

flagged_clauses.customer_proposed_language: Reverberate proposes a 25% revenue share on all Reverberate Engine subscriptions paid by their clients, with no baseline subscription fee, estimated at $400K–$1.2M annual contribution to Clay.
flagged_clauses.risk_explanation: A rev-share-only structure with no floor creates zero guaranteed revenue: if Reverberate fails to convert clients, underreports, or churns, Clay receives nothing despite having extended white-label rights and early access. The wide range ($400K–$1.2M) signals weak financial modeling. Net-45 from quarter close means Clay is effectively extending 135-day credit on Q1 revenue. With no audit right, reported subscription numbers are unverifiable.
flagged_clauses.suggested_counter: Revenue share shall be calculated as 25% of Gross Reverberate Engine Subscription Revenue actually collected by Reverberate from end clients, subject to: (a) a minimum annual guarantee of $150,000 USD payable regardless of actual subscription revenue ('Annual Floor'), pro-rated for partial years; (b) Reverberate providing Clay with a quarterly revenue attestation certified by Reverberate's CFO or equivalent within 30 days of quarter close; (c) Clay retaining the right to audit Reverberate's subscription revenue records no more than once per calendar year upon 15 business days' notice; and (d) remittance due net-30 from end-of-quarter close. If cumulative rev-share payments fail to meet the Annual Floor by December 31 of each contract year, Reverberate shall remit the shortfall within 30 days.
flagged_clauses.fallback_position: Accept no Annual Floor if Reverberate agrees to (i) net-30 remittance, (ii) certified quarterly attestations, and (iii) annual audit rights. Minimum: audit rights and certified attestations are non-negotiable.
flagged_clauses.precedent_notes: No prior rev-share deal exists at Clay; standard deals are subscription-only. This structure is novel and requires Finance and Legal approval.

flagged_clauses.customer_proposed_language: Reverberate requests early access to all new Clay features 30 days before public launch.
flagged_clauses.risk_explanation: Broad 30-day advance access to all new features creates an obligation that can delay Clay's go-to-market sequencing: if Reverberate's early access is contractually required, Clay cannot do a staged rollout, beta-partner prioritization, or a surprise launch for any feature during the 24-month term. It also exposes pre-release product to Reverberate clients who are not under NDA with Clay, creating competitive intelligence risk.
flagged_clauses.suggested_counter: Clay shall use commercially reasonable efforts to provide Reverberate with early access to features designated by Clay as 'Partner Preview' releases, no fewer than 14 calendar days prior to general availability. Early access is subject to: (a) Clay's sole discretion on which features qualify for Partner Preview designation; (b) Reverberate executing Clay's standard Beta Access NDA prior to each Preview release; and (c) exclusion of features subject to third-party embargo, security patches, regulatory requirements, or features developed jointly with other customers. Clay makes no warranty as to feature completeness or stability during Partner Preview periods.
flagged_clauses.fallback_position: Accept 30-day advance access limited to features Clay affirmatively designates as 'Partner Preview,' with NDA requirement and explicit carve-outs for security, regulatory, and embargoed features.
flagged_clauses.precedent_notes: Clay offers beta access to select customers but no contractual advance-access SLA has been documented in prior agreements.

flagged_clauses.customer_proposed_language: Reverberate requests co-marketing rights including the ability to publish case studies that attribute client outcomes to Reverberate Engine, without explicit Clay approval workflow described.
flagged_clauses.risk_explanation: Co-marketing rights without an approval gate allow Reverberate to publish case studies referencing Clay's underlying technology (or omitting it) in ways that could misrepresent product capabilities, violate Clay's agreements with the referenced end clients, or create liability if performance claims are exaggerated. Since the end clients are also Clay's top-50 customers, unauthorized case study publication could damage those direct relationships.
flagged_clauses.suggested_counter: Each co-marketing asset, including case studies, blog posts, and social content, that references Clay technology, the Reverberate Engine partnership, or outcomes attributable to Clay's platform shall require Clay's written approval no fewer than 5 business days prior to publication. Clay's approval shall not be unreasonably withheld. Reverberate shall obtain written consent from each referenced end client prior to submission to Clay for approval. Case studies shall include 'Powered by Clay' attribution unless Clay expressly waives this requirement in writing for a specific asset.
flagged_clauses.fallback_position: Accept a 3-business-day approval turnaround with deemed-approval after 5 business days of silence, provided end-client consent is confirmed in writing before submission.

redline.one_line_summary: We cannot execute this partnership as proposed — we need a minimum revenue floor with audit rights on the rev-share, 'Powered by Clay' attribution embedded in the white-label grant, scoped early access limited to Clay-designated Partner Preview features only, and a co-marketing approval gate before any case study goes live.
redline.reasoning_summary: This is a structurally novel deal for Clay — a white-label partnership with rev-share-only economics, no baseline fee, and broad early-access obligations across a 24-month term. The headline risk is the combination of an unrestricted white-label grant (which could invisibilize Clay's brand with 8 top-50 customers) and a rev-share structure with no floor or audit right (zero revenue guarantee on a $0 ACV contract). Early access as drafted creates a contractual obligation that could constrain every Clay product launch for two years. All four non-standard clauses require legal and executive sign-off; none can be accepted as proposed. Exa signals returned no material intelligence on Reverberate Growth specifically, so leverage assessment relies entirely on AE context: Reverberate is a rebuild risk converted to distribution leverage, which supports a firm but collaborative counter rather than a walk posture.

required_approvers.rationale: Deal type is partnership with rev-share economics; partnership deals fall outside the standard pricing matrix and require CEO + Strategic Partnership Review per matrix rule.

required_approvers.rule_triggered: Partnership / rev-share routes to CEO (legal required for flagged clauses)
required_approvers.rationale: Redline Agent flagged four high/medium-risk non-standard clauses (white_label, rev_share, early_access, co_marketing) at block priority; Legal must negotiate scoped white-label grant, audit rights, and approval gates before execution.

required_approvers.rule_triggered: Partnership / rev-share routes to CEO (CFO required for margin block)
required_approvers.rationale: Pricing Agent flagged gross margin absolute floor as block_absolute because proposed_price is $0 and all consideration is variable rev-share; CFO must pre-clear revenue treatment, principal-vs-agent conclusion, and ASC 606 disclosure before close. ASC 606 Agent also flagged 'no baseline fee — 100% variable consideration' at block_without_approval severity.

required_approvers.rule_triggered: Partnership / rev-share routes to CEO (RevOps required per customer routing note)
required_approvers.rationale: AE notes indicate deal was 'routed to us by RevOps' — RevOps has already engaged and must remain in approval chain to confirm deal structure, customer segment alignment (PLG/self-serve), and partnership precedent implications.
AE Manager (Sarah Goldstein) is the deal owner's manager and must be first step in approval chain regardless of deal type.

blockers_to_address_first: Legal must redline white-label clause to scope IP to Tables/Waterfalls only, add 'Powered by Clay' attribution requirement, and prohibit Reverberate from claiming IP as proprietary.

CFO/Accounting must issue a memo confirming ASC 606 revenue treatment (sales-based royalty exception), principal-vs-agent determination, and COGS attribution methodology for white-label infrastructure before close.

Finance must redline rev-share to add minimum annual guarantee (recommended $120K–$150K floor) and mandatory audit rights; at minimum, quarterly CFO-certified attestations and annual audit rights are non-negotiable per Redline Agent.

Legal must scope early access to Clay-designated Partner Preview features only (14 days minimum, not 30 days), require NDA for each preview release, and carve out security/regulatory/embargoed features.

Legal must add co-marketing approval gate: Clay written approval required 5 business days prior to publication of any case study, blog, or social content; end-client consent required before submission.

Pricing Agent's margin block_absolute must be cleared: either introduce a minimum guaranteed fee ($120K–$150K platform access fee recommended by Pricing Agent) or accept tiered rev-share with minimum quarterly true-up payments to achieve 25%+ margin floor.

AE must obtain Reverberate's written acknowledgment of the 8 overlapping top-50 customers and confirm channel conflict mitigation strategy (e.g., co-sell vs. exclusivity gates) with RevOps and CEO before routing.
approval.one_line_summary: This routes to AE Manager → Legal + CFO + RevOps (parallel) → CEO. Expect ~12 business days. Deal cannot proceed until Legal negotiates white-label scope + attribution, CFO clears rev-share floor + revenue treatment, Finance secures audit rights + attestations, and Pricing resolves margin block with a guaranteed fee or tiered rev-share; all four redline clauses are blockers in their current form.
approval.reasoning_summary: The deal_type is 'partnership' which triggers the matrix's default 'Partnership / rev-share routes to CEO' rule; this is a structural rule that overrides standard pricing/discount gates. Pricing, ASC 606, and Redline agents all flagged independent block-severity issues: margin_absolute floor (Pricing), no baseline fee with 100% variable consideration (ASC 606), and four high/medium-risk non-standard clauses (Redline). The approval chain is standard for executive-level partnership deals with legal and financial complexity: AE Manager first, then Legal+CFO+RevOps in parallel (Legal has veto over IP scope and contract terms; CFO has veto over revenue/margin treatment and rev-share floor; RevOps advises on segment fit and precedent), then CEO for final strategic/brand decision. The 12-day estimate accounts for parallel review at step 2 (max 5 days for Legal/CFO/RevOps) plus CEO review (2 days), plus a 5-day contingency for redline iteration. Confidence is high because the matrix rule is unambiguous (deal_type='partnership' → default route to CEO) and all downstream blockers are explicitly documented by upstream agents.

text.text: Deal Review: Reverberate Engine White-Label Partnership

*Customer:* Reverberate Growth (GTM Agency, PLG/Self-Serve)
*ACV:* $0 subscription | Est. $400K–$1.2M/yr rev-share (25% of Reverberate Engine ARR)
*TCV:* $0 contracted | Est. $800K–$2.4M over 24-month term (midpoint)
*Effective Discount:* N/A — rev-share-only structure, no list price
*Margin:* 0% computed (block_absolute fired); margin-positive only after rev-share remittances exceed white-label COGS — requires CFO clearance
*Approval Chain:* AE Manager (Sarah Goldstein) → Legal + CFO + RevOps (parallel, ~5 days) → CEO | Est. 12 business days

*What the deal team needs to know:*

• *This deal is blocked and cannot proceed as proposed.* All four non-standard clauses (white-label grant, rev-share terms, early access, co-marketing) require redlines before execution. Minimum non-negotiables: scoped white-label to Tables/Waterfalls only with "Powered by Clay" attribution, a minimum annual revenue floor ($120K–$150K recommended) with CFO-certified quarterly attestations and annual audit rights, and a co-marketing approval gate. Legal and Finance must issue redlines before routing to CEO.

• *The gross margin absolute floor (≥25%) is triggered.* With $0 subscription revenue, computed margin is 0% — a block_absolute condition. CFO must pre-clear the ASC 606 revenue treatment (sales-based royalty exception under 606-10-55-65), the principal-vs-agent determination (Clay is likely licensor/agent, not principal — only 25% rev-share hits Clay's top line), and COGS attribution for white-label infrastructure before close. Pricing Agent's recommended fix: introduce a $120K/yr minimum platform fee.

• *8 of Clay's top-50 customers are Reverberate clients — channel conflict must be addressed first.* An unrestricted white-label grant could invisibilize Clay's brand across a material portion of the install base and cannibalize direct ARR. AE Devon Wright must obtain Reverberate's written acknowledgment of the overlap and confirm a channel conflict mitigation strategy with RevOps and CEO before the deal is routed up the approval chain.

elements.text: *AE Owner:* Devon Wright  |  *AE Manager:* Sarah Goldstein  |  *Close Target:* 2026-06-01  |  review filed by Kiln · agent-driven

slack_post.plaintext_fallback:
DEAL REVIEW: Reverberate Engine White-Label Partnership | Customer: Reverberate Growth (PLG/Self-Serve GTM Agency)
ACV: $0 contracted; est. $400K–$1.2M/yr via 25% rev-share. No subscription fee. Gross margin block_absolute fired — CFO must clear revenue treatment and minimum fee structure before close.
Approval chain: AE Manager (Sarah Goldstein) → Legal + CFO + RevOps (parallel) → CEO. Est. 12 business days.
Top blockers: (1) All 4 non-standard clauses blocked — Legal must redline white-label scope/attribution, rev-share floor + audit rights, early access carve-outs, and co-marketing approval gate. (2) CFO must issue ASC 606 memo (principal-vs-agent, royalty exception) and clear margin floor. (3) Channel conflict across 8 shared top-50 customers must be resolved before routing.
AE Owner: Devon Wright | review filed by Kiln · agent-driven

ae_email_draft.subject: [Reverberate] Deal-desk review complete — 3 blockers before this can move
ae_email_draft.body_markdown:
Devon, the deal-desk review is done and this deal is **blocked from routing** until three things are resolved on your end.

1. **Get Reverberate to accept a minimum annual guarantee.** The $0 ACV / pure rev-share structure trips a hard margin floor. Finance's recommended floor is $120K–$150K/yr; bring this to Reverberate as a commercial ask before Legal opens the redline.
2. **Flag the 8 overlapping top-50 customers to RevOps and get a channel-conflict position in writing.** CEO will ask about this on first look — have the answer ready before you route.
3. **Do not send any contract draft to Reverberate yet.** All four non-standard clauses (white-label scope, rev-share, early access, co-marketing) are redlined at block priority; Legal owns the next version of the paper.

Once those three items are addressed, route the deal as follows: **Sarah Goldstein (your manager) → Legal + CFO + RevOps in parallel → CEO**. Budget ~12 business days for the full cycle and expect Legal and CFO each hold veto rights at step 2.

customer_email_draft.subject: Reverberate Engine Partnership — Clay's Position on the Four Open Points
customer_email_draft.body_markdown:
We appreciate the thoughtfulness behind the Reverberate Engine proposal, and we genuinely see the mutual upside here — your distribution reach and our infrastructure are a natural fit. That said, our internal review has surfaced four areas where we need to land in a different place before we can move this forward.

On the **white-label grant**: we can support white-labeling Tables and Waterfalls for active Reverberate client engagements, but we need 'Powered by Clay' attribution in-product and an explicit prohibition on representing the underlying technology as proprietary to Reverberate. We're open to discussing attribution placement, but this is a firm requirement.

On the **rev-share structure**: a $0 floor with no audit rights isn't a structure we can execute — our Finance team requires either a minimum annual guarantee (we're proposing $120K–$150K, creditable against rev-share above a gross threshold) or, at minimum, quarterly CFO-certified revenue attestations plus annual audit rights. The net-45 remittance window should move to net-30.

On **early access**: we can commit to Partner Preview access no fewer than 14 days before general availability for features Clay designates for partner release, with a mutual NDA governing each preview — but we can't contractually obligate 30-day advance access across all features, as that would constrain our launch sequencing for the full 24-month term.

On **co-marketing**: we're excited about joint case studies and are prepared to move quickly on approvals (5 business days, with deemed approval after silence), but each asset referencing Clay technology or client outcomes will need written end-client consent and Clay sign-off before publication.

This deal requires CEO, CFO, and Legal approval on our side, which we're actively routing — our expected internal cycle is approximately 12 business days. To keep your June 1 target in reach, the most useful next step is a 45-minute call with your founder, Devon, and our Head of Legal to align on the white-label scope and rev-share floor before we exchange a redlined term sheet.

sections.heading: Headline
sections.content_markdown: Reverberate Growth, a top-50 GTM agency, proposes a 24-month white-label partnership: they resell Clay tables and waterfalls as "Reverberate Engine" to their clients, paying Clay a **25% rev share** (est. $400K–$1.2M/yr) with **no baseline subscription fee**. ACV/TCV are $0 on contract; all economics are variable. Deal converts a potential rebuild-and-compete risk into a distribution channel covering 8 of Clay's own top-50 customers.

sections.heading: Pricing Summary
sections.content_markdown:
| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| List / Proposed Price | $0 (rev-share only) |
| Effective Discount | N/A — no priced contract |
| Estimated Annual Rev-Share | $400K–$1.2M (midpoint ~$800K) |
| Guaranteed Revenue | **$0** — 100% variable |
| Computed Margin | **0%** (block_absolute fired) |

**Margin guardrail status:** Gross margin absolute floor (≥25%) is a **hard block** — no fixed revenue line exists to certify margin compliance. CFO must resolve before close.

**Recommended alternative structures (from Pricing Agent):**
- **Option A — Minimum Guarantee + Rev-Share Blend:** $120K/yr non-refundable platform fee offset against rev-share above $480K gross threshold → clears margin block at ~40% on the floor.
- **Option B — Tiered Rev-Share (20% / 28% / 33%):** No floor; ~7% ACV uplift at midpoint but margin uncertainty persists — must pair with a minimum quarterly true-up.
- **Option C — $75K Platform Access Fee + 18% Rev-Share:** Immediately books subscription revenue at ~40% margin; Reverberate's total cost at $800K gross is ~$219K vs. $200K today — modest premium offset by formalized co-marketing rights.

sections.heading: Risk Findings
sections.content_markdown:
**Redline — Overall Priority: BLOCK (4 flagged clauses)**

| Clause | Risk | Status |
|---|---|---|
| White-label grant | HIGH — unrestricted; Clay brand invisible to 8 top-50 customers; non-transferability absent; sets reseller commoditization precedent | Must redline: scope to Tables/Waterfalls only, add "Powered by Clay" in-product attribution, prohibit Reverberate from claiming IP as proprietary, add audit right |
| Rev-share (25%, no floor) | HIGH — zero guaranteed revenue; no audit rights; effectively 135-day credit on Q1 revenue; unverifiable subscription numbers | Must add: $120K–$150K annual floor, CFO-certified quarterly attestations, annual audit right, net-30 remittance |
| Early access (30-day blanket) | MEDIUM — contractual obligation constrains every Clay product launch for 24 months; exposes pre-release product without NDA | Redline to: Clay-designated "Partner Preview" only, ≥14 days, NDA per release, carve-outs for security/regulatory/embargoed features |
| Co-marketing (no approval gate) | MEDIUM — Reverberate can publish case studies referencing top-50 clients without Clay review, risking relationship damage and misrepresentation | Add: 5-day written approval gate, end-client consent required before submission, "Powered by Clay" attribution |

**ASC 606 Red Flags**

- 🔴 **BLOCK:** 100% variable consideration ($0 fixed) — CFO and auditor must pre-clear revenue treatment, principal-vs-agent conclusion (Clay is likely licensor/agent; only 25% rev-share hits Clay's top line), and disclosure approach before signing.
- 🟡 **WARN:** Principal-vs-agent determination requires formal accounting memo — end customers contract with Reverberate, not Clay.
- 🟡 **WARN:** No audit-rights clause → quarterly remittance unverifiable; revenue cutoff risk at quarter-end.
- 🟡 **WARN:** Channel conflict — white-label could cannibalize direct ARR from 8 overlapping top-50 customers; affects standalone selling price analysis.
- 🔵 **INFO:** Co-marketing and early-access promises require distinctness assessment to confirm they are not separate performance obligations under ASC 606-10-25-19.

sections.heading: Approval Routing
sections.content_markdown:
| Step | Approver | Veto? | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **AE Manager** — Sarah Goldstein | No | Initiating step; must also confirm channel conflict mitigation strategy with RevOps |
| 2 (parallel) | **Legal** | ✅ Yes | Negotiate all 4 redlined clauses; white-label scope and attribution are non-negotiable minimums |
| 2 (parallel) | **CFO** | ✅ Yes | Issue ASC 606 memo (sales-based royalty exception + principal-vs-agent); clear margin block_absolute; approve rev-share floor structure |
| 2 (parallel) | **RevOps** | No | Confirm PLG/self-serve segment fit, partnership precedent implications, deal structure sign-off |
| 3 | **CEO** | ✅ Yes | Final strategic/brand decision; partnership deals default to CEO per approval matrix |

**Estimated cycle time: ~12 business days** (5 days parallel Legal/CFO/RevOps review + 2 days CEO + 5 days redline iteration buffer)

**Deal cannot route to Step 2 until:** AE confirms written acknowledgment of 8 overlapping top-50 customers and channel conflict mitigation strategy.

sections.heading: Recommendation
sections.content_markdown:
**🟡 SHIP WITH CONDITIONS**

The partnership has genuine strategic merit — converting a rebuild risk into distribution leverage across 8 top-50 customers — but the deal **cannot be executed as proposed**: all four redline clauses are blockers, the gross margin absolute floor is a hard block, and ASC 606 revenue treatment requires CFO pre-clearance; the minimum conditions to proceed are a guaranteed revenue floor ($120K–$150K/yr platform fee or equivalent), scoped white-label with "Powered by Clay" attribution, audit rights on rev-share remittance, and a co-marketing approval gate — ship once these are contractually secured.

comms.reasoning_summary: Calibrated customer email to collaborative tone given the deal context. AE email leads with verdict (ship-with-conditions (blockers must clear first)) and a numbered next-step list. One-pager and Slack post both headline the recommendation and surface 4 flagged redlines (4 countered in customer reply). Approval routing summary: ~12 business days through 5-step chain.

synthesis: Pricing cannot be evaluated against standard guardrails because the deal carries $0 ACV and no subscription line, but the gross-margin absolute floor technically fires and CFO must validate white-label COGS before any structure is approved. ASC 606 treatment hinges on the sales-based royalty exception with 100% variable consideration, plus a live principal-vs-agent question and contract-modification risk over the 24-month term — final call requires CFO and auditor sign-off. Redline is the loudest blocker: an unrestricted white-label grant risks invisibilizing Clay's brand with up to 8 top-50 customers, and the rev-share has no floor or audit right, so all four non-standard clauses must be countered before close. Approval routes through the partnership rule directly to CEO via AE Manager → Legal/CFO/RevOps (parallel) → CEO, an estimated 12 business days. Recommendation is ship-with-conditions on a collaborative tone — counter the four redlines firmly, secure a rev-share floor and audit right, and do not accept as drafted.
